name: JSON Formatter
intro: Format, beautify, and validate JSON online.
description: Use this free JSON formatter to clean up minified JSON, improve readability, and quickly validate JSON structure.

faq.q: What does a JSON formatter do?
faq.a: A JSON formatter makes raw JSON easier to read by adding indentation and line breaks.

faq.q: Can I validate JSON with this tool?
faq.a: Yes. A JSON formatter can also help detect invalid JSON structure.

name: JSON Validator
intro: Validate JSON structure online.
description: Check whether your JSON is valid and catch syntax issues quickly with this free JSON validator.

faq.q: What is a JSON validator?
faq.a: A JSON validator checks whether JSON syntax is correct and properly structured.

faq.q: Can this tool find JSON errors?
faq.a: Yes. It helps identify invalid JSON formatting and syntax mistakes.

name: JSON Minifier
intro: Minify JSON by removing spaces and line breaks.
description: Compress JSON into a compact single-line format for storage, transfer, or embedding.

faq.q: What does a JSON minifier do?
faq.a: It removes whitespace, indentation, and line breaks from JSON to reduce size.

faq.q: Will JSON still work after minifying?
faq.a: Yes. Valid JSON remains valid after minification.

name: Base64 Encoder
intro: Encode plain text to Base64 format online.
description: Convert plain text into Base64 instantly with this free online Base64 encoder.

faq.q: What is Base64 encoding?
faq.a: Base64 encoding converts text or binary data into an ASCII string format.

faq.q: Is Base64 encryption?
faq.a: No. Base64 is encoding, not encryption.

name: Base64 Decoder
intro: Decode Base64 text back into readable text.
description: Use this free Base64 decoder to convert Base64 strings back into plain text.

faq.q: What does a Base64 decoder do?
faq.a: It converts Base64-encoded text back into its original readable form.

faq.q: Can I decode Base64 online for free?
faq.a: Yes. This tool is free to use online.

name: URL Encoder
intro: Encode text for safe use in URLs.
description: Convert special characters into URL-safe encoding for query strings, links, and parameters.

faq.q: Why do I need URL encoding?
faq.a: URL encoding makes special characters safe to use inside URLs and query parameters.

faq.q: Does URL encoding change text meaning?
faq.a: No. It only changes how special characters are represented in URLs.

name: URL Decoder
intro: Decode URL-encoded text into readable form.
description: Convert percent-encoded URL strings back into plain readable text instantly.

faq.q: What does a URL decoder do?
faq.a: It converts encoded URL characters like %20 back into normal readable text.

faq.q: Can I decode query parameters with this tool?
faq.a: Yes. It is useful for decoding links and query strings.

name: HTML Encoder
intro: Encode special characters into HTML entities.
description: Convert characters like <, >, and & into HTML-safe entities for code and content.

faq.q: Why use HTML encoding?
faq.a: HTML encoding prevents special characters from being interpreted as HTML markup.

faq.q: What characters are usually encoded?
faq.a: Common examples include angle brackets, ampersands, and quotes.

name: HTML Decoder
intro: Decode HTML entities into normal text.
description: Convert HTML entities like & and < back into readable characters.

faq.q: What does an HTML decoder do?
faq.a: It converts encoded HTML entities back into their original characters.

faq.q: Is this useful for copied HTML text?
faq.a: Yes. It helps when text contains entity codes instead of symbols.

name: MD5 Hash Generator
intro: Generate an MD5 hash from text.
description: Create MD5 hashes from input strings for testing, checksums, and quick comparisons.

faq.q: What is an MD5 hash?
faq.a: MD5 is a hashing algorithm that converts input text into a fixed-length hash value.

faq.q: Is MD5 secure for passwords?
faq.a: No. MD5 is outdated and should not be used for password storage.

name: SHA256 Hash Generator
intro: Generate a SHA256 hash from text.
description: Convert input text into a SHA256 hash for checksums, testing, and development tasks.

faq.q: What is SHA256 used for?
faq.a: SHA256 is commonly used for checksums, integrity checks, and cryptographic workflows.

faq.q: Can two different inputs have the same SHA256 hash?
faq.a: In practice this is extremely unlikely.

name: UUID Generator
intro: Generate random UUIDs online.
description: Create unique identifiers for apps, databases, APIs, and development workflows.

faq.q: What is a UUID?
faq.a: A UUID is a universally unique identifier used to label records and objects.

faq.q: Why use a UUID generator?
faq.a: It helps create unique IDs without needing a central counter.

name: IP Lookup
intro: Check basic information about an IP address.
description: Look up useful IP address details with this simple online IP lookup tool.

faq.q: What is an IP lookup tool?
faq.a: An IP lookup tool helps you inspect information related to an IP address.

faq.q: Can I use this IP lookup tool for free?
faq.a: Yes. It is free to use.

name: DNS Lookup
intro: Check DNS records for a domain.
description: Inspect domain DNS information such as A, AAAA, MX, TXT, and CNAME records.

faq.q: What is a DNS lookup?
faq.a: A DNS lookup checks the DNS records associated with a domain.

faq.q: What records can be checked?
faq.a: Common records include A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS, and CNAME.

name: Ping Test
intro: Test basic host reachability online.
description: Use a ping test tool to check whether a host responds and estimate network latency.

faq.q: What does a ping test do?
faq.a: It checks whether a host is reachable and measures response time.

faq.q: Can ping fail even if a site is up?
faq.a: Yes. Some servers block ICMP ping responses.

name: Port Checker
intro: Check whether a port is open on a host.
description: Test common TCP ports and verify whether a target host is listening on a specific port.

faq.q: What is a port checker?
faq.a: A port checker tests whether a specific network port is open and reachable.

faq.q: Why would a port appear closed?
faq.a: It may be blocked by a firewall, filtered, or not have a listening service.

name: Word Counter
intro: Count words, characters, and lines in text.

faq.q: What does a word counter measure?

faq.q: Can I use a word counter for essays?
faq.a: Yes. It is useful for essays, articles, and social media drafts.

faq.q: Does it count spaces?

name: Remove Extra Spaces
intro: Clean text by removing unnecessary spaces.
description: Remove duplicate spaces and tidy messy text formatting with one click.

faq.q: What does this tool do?
faq.a: It removes repeated or unnecessary spaces from text.

faq.q: Is it useful for copied text?
faq.a: Yes. It helps clean text copied from PDFs, websites, and documents.

name: Text Case Converter

faq.q: Who uses a text case converter?

name: Timestamp Converter

faq.q: What is a Unix timestamp?
faq.a: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds since January 1, 1970 UTC.

name: Bytes to MB Converter

faq.q: What is Base64 URL encoding?

faq.q: Is this the same as normal Base64?

faq.a: ROT13 is a simple letter substitution that rotates alphabet characters by 13 positions.

faq.a: Yes. Applying ROT13 twice returns the original text.

faq.q: What date format should I use?
faq.a: Use YYYY-MM-DD format like 2026-03-22 on each line for the most reliable result.

faq.a: No. The tool sorts them automatically and returns the absolute difference.

name: Sentence Counter

description: Use this sentence counter to measure how many sentences your text contains. It is useful for writing checks, content editing, reading exercises, and text analysis.

faq.q: How are sentences counted?
faq.a: The tool splits text by common sentence-ending punctuation such as periods, exclamation marks, and question marks.

faq.a: Yes. It helps estimate content structure and readability when drafting articles, descriptions, or landing pages.

name: Title Case Converter
intro: Convert text to title case for headlines and titles.
description: This title case converter helps you format text for headings, page titles, article names, and UI labels. It is useful for content editing, SEO titles, and general text cleanup.

faq.q: What is title case?
faq.a: Title case means each main word begins with a capital letter, which is common for headings and titles.

faq.q: Can I use it for SEO titles?
faq.a: Yes. It is useful when formatting page titles, blog headlines, and metadata text.

name: Remove Line Breaks
intro: Remove line breaks and turn multi-line text into one clean line.
description: Use this tool to remove line breaks from pasted text and turn paragraphs or lists into a single clean line. It is useful for forms, spreadsheets, CMS fields, and data cleanup.

faq.q: What happens to paragraph breaks?
faq.a: The tool replaces line breaks with spaces and collapses extra spacing so the result stays readable.

faq.q: When is this useful?
faq.a: It is useful when pasting content into one-line form fields, CSV cells, databases, or admin panels.

name: Text Splitter
intro: Split text into separate lines using a custom separator.
description: This text splitter helps you break a string into multiple lines based on a separator such as comma, pipe, slash, or semicolon. It is useful for cleaning lists, imports, and structured text.

faq.q: How do I set the separator?
faq.a: Put the separator on the first line, leave a blank line, then paste the text you want to split.

faq.q: Can I split CSV-like data?
faq.a: Yes. You can use a comma, semicolon, pipe, slash, or any custom separator.

faq.q: What separators can I use?

name: HEX to RGB

faq.a: Enter three numbers for red, green, and blue, such as 255 87 51.

faq.a: REM units help create scalable layouts and typography that adapt better across devices.

name: Loan Calculator
intro: Estimate monthly loan payments, total payment, and total interest.
description: This loan calculator helps you estimate monthly payments based on loan amount, annual interest rate, and term length. It is useful for personal budgeting, credit planning, and comparing borrowing options.

faq.q: What loan details do I need?
faq.a: Enter the loan amount, annual interest rate, and loan term in months. The tool returns estimated monthly payment, total payment, and total interest.

faq.q: Does this work for zero interest loans?
faq.a: Yes. If the interest rate is 0, the tool simply divides the loan amount by the number of months.

faq.a: Enter the start time on the first line and the end time on the second line in HH:MM format.

faq.q: Can the calculator handle overnight times?
faq.a: Yes. If the end time is earlier than the start time, the tool treats it as the next day.

name: MB to Bytes
intro: Convert megabytes to bytes instantly.

faq.q: What standard does the tool use?
faq.a: The converter uses 1 MB = 1,048,576 bytes, which is based on 1024.

faq.q: Can I enter decimal values?
faq.a: Yes. You can convert whole numbers or decimal MB values.

name: Mbps to MB/s
intro: Convert megabits per second to megabytes per second.
description: This Mbps to MB/s converter helps you compare internet speed values with file transfer speeds. It is useful for networking, downloads, uploads, and bandwidth planning.

faq.q: Why are Mbps and MB/s different?
faq.a: Mbps measures megabits per second, while MB/s measures megabytes per second. One byte equals eight bits.

faq.q: When is this useful?
faq.a: It is useful when comparing ISP speeds with actual download or transfer rates.

name: MB/s to Mbps
intro: Convert megabytes per second to megabits per second.
description: Use this MB/s to Mbps converter to turn file transfer speed into network speed format. It is useful for testing bandwidth, comparing ISP plans, and understanding download performance.

faq.q: What is the conversion rule?
faq.a: To convert MB/s to Mbps, multiply the value by 8.

faq.q: Does this help with internet speed tests?
faq.a: Yes. It helps compare storage-style speed readings with network-style speed units.

name: RGB to HEX
intro: Convert RGB values to HEX color format quickly.

faq.q: How should I enter RGB values?
faq.a: Enter red, green, and blue values separated by spaces or commas, such as 255 87 51.

faq.q: What range is allowed?

faq.q: How do I enter HSL values?

faq.q: Do I need the percent symbol?

faq.q: What HTML does this support?

faq.q: Who is this useful for?

name: Remove Line Breaks
intro: Remove line breaks and turn text into one clean line.
description: This remove line breaks tool helps you convert multi-line text into one single clean line. It is useful for forms, spreadsheets, CMS fields, and data cleanup.

faq.q: What happens to paragraphs?
faq.a: Line breaks are replaced with spaces so the result stays readable.

faq.q: When is this useful?
faq.a: It is useful when pasting text into one-line fields, cells, or systems that do not support multiple lines.

name: Hash Generator
intro: Generate common hashes from text instantly.
description: Use this hash generator to create MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hashes from text. It is useful for development, testing, checksums, and quick data verification.

faq.q: What hashes does this tool generate?
faq.a: The tool generates MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 from the text you enter.

faq.q: Who is this useful for?
faq.a: It is useful for developers, testers, sysadmins, and anyone checking or comparing text hashes.

name: Percentage Change Calculator
intro: Calculate percentage increase or decrease between two values.
description: This percentage change calculator helps you compare an original value and a new value to see the exact percentage increase or decrease. It is useful for finance, analytics, pricing, and reporting.

faq.q: How do I use this calculator?
faq.a: Enter the original value on the first line and the new value on the second line. The tool returns the percentage change.

faq.q: Can it show decreases?
faq.a: Yes. If the second number is lower than the first, the result is shown as a negative percentage.

name: Average Calculator
intro: Calculate the average of multiple numbers quickly.
description: Use this average calculator to find the mean value from a list of numbers. It is useful for grades, analytics, finance, metrics, and everyday calculations.

faq.q: How do I enter values?
faq.a: Enter numbers separated by spaces, commas, or line breaks.

faq.q: What average does this calculate?
faq.a: It calculates the arithmetic mean.

name: Ratio Calculator
intro: Simplify and compare ratios instantly.
description: This ratio calculator helps you simplify ratios and view their decimal form. It is useful for math, design scaling, recipes, and proportional comparisons.

faq.q: How do I enter a ratio?
faq.a: Enter two numbers separated by a colon, slash, space, comma, or line break.

faq.q: What does the tool return?
faq.a: It returns the simplified ratio and the decimal value of the first number divided by the second.

name: Text Splitter
intro: Split text into separate lines using a custom separator.
description: Use this text splitter to break a string into separate lines based on a comma, pipe, slash, semicolon, or other custom separator. It is useful for cleanup, imports, and list formatting.

faq.q: How do I choose the separator?
faq.a: Put the separator on the first line, leave a blank line, then paste the text you want to split.

faq.q: Can I split CSV-like text?
faq.a: Yes. It works well for simple comma-separated or delimiter-separated values.

name: Text Joiner
intro: Join multiple lines into one line with a custom separator.
description: This text joiner helps you combine many lines into one string with a separator like a comma, pipe, semicolon, or space. It is useful for tags, code snippets, SQL lists, and bulk formatting.

faq.q: How do I choose the separator?
faq.a: Put the separator on the first line, leave a blank line, then paste one item per line.

faq.q: Can I use custom separators?
faq.a: Yes. You can use commas, spaces, pipes, semicolons, tabs, or custom text.

name: Subnet Calculator
intro: Calculate network details from an IPv4 address and CIDR prefix.
description: Use this subnet calculator to find the network address, broadcast address, usable host range, and total hosts for an IPv4 subnet. It is useful for networking, labs, exams, and planning.

faq.q: How do I use this subnet calculator?
faq.a: Enter an IPv4 address and CIDR prefix like 192.168.1.10/24.

faq.q: What does the tool return?
faq.a: It returns the network address, broadcast address, subnet mask, and usable host range.

name: CIDR Calculator
intro: Convert CIDR prefixes into subnet masks and host counts.
description: This CIDR calculator helps you convert an IPv4 CIDR prefix into a subnet mask, wildcard mask, and host capacity. It is useful for networking, certifications, subnet planning, and quick reference.

faq.q: How do I use this CIDR calculator?
faq.a: Enter a prefix like /24 or just 24, and the tool will return subnet details.

faq.q: What does the tool show?
faq.a: It shows the subnet mask, wildcard mask, total addresses, and usable hosts.
